
```{r}
set.seed(0)
pacman::p_load(cmdstanr, tidyverse, cowplot, posterior, ggh4x, ggpmisc, tidybayes, loo, bayesplot, patchwork)
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
```

```{r}

## Simulating the two agents

agent.PBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + (1 - own_weighting) * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + (1 - own_weighting) * (8 - external_rating)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})

agent.WBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + external_weighting * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + external_weighting * (8 - external_rating)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})

simulate <- function(
    agent_evaluator,
    n_observations,
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
  tibble(
    FaceID=seq(1, n_observations),
    FirstRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
    GroupRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
  ) |> mutate(
    SecondRating=agent_evaluator(
        alpha_prior=alpha_prior,
        beta_prior=beta_prior,
        own_rating=FirstRating,
        external_rating=GroupRating,
        own_weighting=own_weighting,
        external_weighting=external_weighting
    ),
  )
}

simulation <- simulate(
  agent_evaluator=agent.PBA,
  n_observations=50,
  alpha_prior=0.5,
  beta_prior=0.5,
  own_weighting=0.5,
  external_weighting=NA
) |> mutate(
  ID=1,
  model=""
)

add_diagnostic_columns <- function(data) {
  data |> mutate(
    Feedback=GroupRating-FirstRating,
    Change=SecondRating-FirstRating,
  )
}

plot_feedback_change_relation <- function(data, title="", alpha=0.5) 
{
  ggplot(data, aes(x=Feedback, y=Change)) +
    geom_jitter(width=0.1, height=0.1, alpha=alpha, shape=1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7, 2), limits=c(-7.5, 7.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7, 2), limits=c(-7.5, 7.5)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept=0, alpha=0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=0, alpha=0.2) +
    stat_correlation(small.r=TRUE) +
    geom_smooth(method=lm, formula=y~x, se=TRUE) +
    labs(title=title) +
    coord_fixed() +
    facet_grid(rows=vars(ID), cols=vars(model))
}

plot_feedback_change_relation(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))
```



```{r fig.width = 10}

## Participant change 

plot_participant_behaviour <- function(data) {
  ggplot(data) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating, yend=SecondRating), 
    arrow=arrow(type="closed", length = unit(0.2, "cm"))
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating), 
    color="black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=GroupRating),
    color="red"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  facet_grid(rows=vars(ID)) +
  labs(y="Ratings") # arrow points to second rating, red denotes group rating
}

plot_participant_behaviour(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))

```


```{r}

## Plotting the ratings, feedback and change

add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(FirstRating, GroupRating, SecondRating),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Rating",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Rating)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  facet_grid(rows=vars(ID), cols=vars(Observation)) +
  labs(y="Observation count")

add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(Feedback, Change),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Offset",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Offset)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7), limits=c(-7, 7)) +
  facet_grid(rows=vars(ID), cols=vars(factor(Observation, c("Feedback", "Change")))) +
  labs(y="Observation count")
```





```{r}

## Setting up agent types

agents_wba <- tibble(
  AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
  own_weighting = c(1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
  external_weighting = c(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)
)

agents_pba <- tibble(
  AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
  own_weighting = c(1, 0.5, 0.25),
  external_weighting = NA
)

## Global params 
n_observations <- 100
alpha_prior    <- 4
beta_prior     <- 3

## adding columns to identify agent type and ModelType in the results
run_sim <- function(agent_evaluator, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType) {
  simulate(
    agent_evaluator    = agent_evaluator,
    n_observations     = n_observations,
    alpha_prior        = alpha_prior,
    beta_prior         = beta_prior,
    own_weighting      = own_weighting,
    external_weighting = external_weighting
  ) |> mutate(
    Feedback   = GroupRating - FirstRating,
    Change     = SecondRating - FirstRating,
    AbsChange  = abs(SecondRating - FirstRating),
    AgentType = AgentType,
    ModelType = ModelType
  )
}

## run for pba typologies
results_pba <- agents_pba |>
  pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
    run_sim(agent.PBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "PBA")
  }) |>
  list_rbind() |> drop_na(SecondRating)

## run for wba typologies
results_wba <- agents_wba |>
  pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
    run_sim(agent.WBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "WBA")
  }) |>
  list_rbind()|> drop_na(SecondRating)

## combine
results_all <- bind_rows(results_pba, results_wba)

```


```{r}

## Plot the combined results for each ModelType and AgentType

for (m in unique(results_all$ModelType)) {
  p <- results_all |>
    filter(ModelType == m) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SecondRating, fill = AgentType)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "dodge", colour = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~ AgentType) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
    labs(title    = paste("Distribution of Second Ratings:", m),
      x = "Second Rating",
      y = "Count",
      fill = "Agent Type") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  print(p)
}

```

```{r}

## Model recovery across PBA and WBA agent typologies
n_sims <- 50 ## EDIT FOR REAL RECOV PLEASE MATTIS

## Function for prepping the data 

prepare_stan_data <- function(df) {
  list(
    N            = nrow(df),
    FirstRating  = as.integer(df$FirstRating),
    GroupRating  = as.integer(df$GroupRating),
    SecondRating = as.integer(df$SecondRating)
  )
}


regenerate_simulations <- TRUE  # set to FALSE to load from disk next time

if (regenerate_simulations || !file.exists("./model_recovery.rds")) {

  ## Build simulation grid 
  ## Cross each agent typology with n_sims replicates
  sim_grid <- bind_rows(
    agents_pba |> mutate(ModelType = "PBA"),
    agents_wba |> mutate(ModelType = "WBA")
  ) |>
    crossing(sim_id = 1:n_sims) 

  ## Fit both candidate models to every simulated dataset
  results_list <- vector("list", nrow(sim_grid))

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sim_grid))) {
    row             <- sim_grid[i, ]
    true_mod        <- row$ModelType
    true_agent_type <- row$AgentType
    true_own        <- row$own_weighting
    true_ext        <- row$external_weighting  # NA for PBA rows
    id              <- row$sim_id

    ## Simulate fresh data 
    agent_fn <- if (true_mod == "PBA") agent.PBA else agent.WBA

    df <- run_sim(
      agent_evaluator    = agent_fn,
      own_weighting      = true_own,
      external_weighting = true_ext,
      AgentType          = true_agent_type,
      ModelType          = true_mod
    )

    stan_data <- prepare_stan_data(df)
    
    ## Init models 
    
    mod_proportional <- cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan")
    mod_weighted     <- cmdstan_model("./stan/WBA.stan")
    

    ## Fit PBA candidate model
    fit_p <- mod_proportional$sample(
      data          = stan_data,
      chains        = 2,
      iter_warmup   = 500,
      iter_sampling = 500,
      refresh       = 0,
      show_messages = FALSE,
      save_warmup   = TRUE
    )

    ## Fit WBA candidate model
    fit_w <- mod_weighted$sample(
      data          = stan_data,
      chains        = 2,
      iter_warmup   = 500,
      iter_sampling = 500,
      refresh       = 0,
      show_messages = FALSE,
      save_warmup   = TRUE
    )

    ## LOO-CV for model comparison
    loo_p <- fit_p$loo()
    loo_w <- fit_w$loo()

    weights <- loo::loo_model_weights(
      list(PBA = loo_p, WBA = loo_w),
      method = "stacking"
    )

    ## Posterior draws
    pba_draws <- fit_p$draws(format = "df")
    wba_draws <- fit_w$draws(format = "df")

    results_list[[i]] <- tibble(
      sim_id             = id,
      true_model         = true_mod,
      true_agent_type    = true_agent_type,
      true_own_weighting = true_own,
      true_ext_weighting = true_ext,          # NA for PBA-generated rows

      ## Stacking weights — primary recovery signal
      weight_PBA = as.numeric(weights["PBA"]),
      weight_WBA = as.numeric(weights["WBA"]),

      ## Recovered parameters from PBA candidate
      ## PBA only has own_weighting (external_weighting = 1 - own_weighting)
      pba_recovered_own = mean(pba_draws$p),
      pba_recovered_ext = 1 - mean(pba_draws$p),

      ## Recovered parameters from WBA candidate
      wba_recovered_own = mean(wba_draws$own_weighting),
      wba_recovered_ext = mean(wba_draws$external_weighting)
    )
  }

  recovery_results <- bind_rows(results_list)
  saveRDS(recovery_results, "./model_recovery.rds")
} else {
  recovery_results <- readRDS("./model_recovery.rds")
}

```


```{r}
## Visualizing the sampling process 

# fit wba

np_w <- nuts_params(fit_w)

# Trace plots
bayesplot::mcmc_trace(
  fit_w$draws(format = "array"),
  pars = c("rho", "kappa"),
  np   = np_w
)


# fit pba 
np_p <- nuts_params(fit_p)


# Trace plots
bayesplot::mcmc_trace(
  fit_p$draws(format = "array"),
  pars = "p",
  np   = np_p
)

cat("PBA divergences:", mean(np_p$Value[np_p$Parameter == "divergent__"]) * 100, "%\n")
cat("WBA divergences:", mean(np_w$Value[np_w$Parameter == "divergent__"]) * 100, "%\n")

```




```{r}

## Visualizations of the model recovery - Riccardos Viz - Currently the models are not very distinguishable, its all just PBA

binary_matrix <- recovery_results |>
  # 1. Reshape to make finding the maximum weight per simulation easier
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("weight_"), 
               names_to = "predicted_model", values_to = "weight") |>
  mutate(predicted_model = str_remove(predicted_model, "weight_")) |>
  
  # 2. Keep only the winning model for each simulation
  group_by(sim_id) |>
  slice_max(weight, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # 3. Count the wins and calculate proportions per true model
  count(true_model, predicted_model) |>
  group_by(true_model) |>
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  
  # 4. Ensure we have all combinations factored correctly (handles 0% scenarios)
  mutate(
    true_model = factor(true_model, levels = c("PBA", "WBA")),
    predicted_model = factor(predicted_model, levels = c("PBA", "WBA"))
  ) |>
  complete(true_model, predicted_model, fill = list(n = 0, proportion = 0))


binary_matrix |>
  ggplot(aes(x = predicted_model, y = true_model, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(proportion, accuracy = 1)),
            size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) + # Keeps SBA at the top left
  labs(
    title = "Model Recovery - Confusion matrix",
    x = "Winning Candidate Model (Max Weight)", 
    y = "True Generating Model",
    fill = "Win Rate"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r fig.height=12}

## visualization of the empirical dataset

cogsci_data_observations <- read_csv("./data/cogsci_clean.csv") |> 
  filter(FirstRating != 0, GroupRating != 0, SecondRating != 0) |> 
  mutate(FaceID = as.integer(factor(FaceID))) |> 
  filter(ID<=5)

plot_participant_behaviour(add_diagnostic_columns(cogsci_data_observations))
```


```{r}

## Fitting to empirical data

get_draws <- function(fit, params) { 
  as_draws_df(fit$draws()) |> 
    as_tibble() |> 
    select(all_of(params)) |> 
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(
      source = if_else(str_detect(parameter, "_prior$"), "Prior", "Posterior"),
      parameter = str_remove(parameter, "_prior$"),
    )
}

fits <- list()
draws <- tibble()
predictions <- tibble()
diagnostics <- tibble()

models <- list(
  PBA=cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE)),
  WBA=cmdstan_model("./stan/WBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE))
)

for (participant_id in unique(cogsci_data_observations$ID)) {
  cogsci_data_observations.participant <- cogsci_data_observations |> filter(ID==participant_id)
  particpant_data = list(
    N=nrow(cogsci_data_observations.participant),
    FirstRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$FirstRating,
    GroupRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$GroupRating,
    SecondRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$SecondRating
  )

  for (model in names(models)) {
    fit <- models[[model]]$sample(
      data=particpant_data,
      seed = 0,
      chains = 4,
      parallel_chains = 4,
      iter_sampling = 1000,
      iter_warmup = 1000,
      show_messages = FALSE,
      show_exceptions = TRUE,
      refresh = 0
    )
    
   
   fits <- append(fits, fit) 
    draws <- bind_rows(draws, mutate(get_draws(fit, params = everything()), model=model, ID=participant_id))
    predictions <- bind_rows(predictions, left_join(mutate(fit |> spread_draws(prior_pred[FaceID], posterior_pred[FaceID]), model=model, ID=participant_id), select(cogsci_data_observations.participant, !ID), by = "FaceID"))
    diagnostics <- bind_rows(diagnostics, mutate(fit$sampler_diagnostics() |> as_draws_df() |> as_tibble() |> select(divergent__, .chain, .iteration, .draw), model=model, ID=participant_id))
  }
}

# OCCASIONAl WARNING:
#Chain 4 Informational Message: The current Metropolis proposal is about to be rejected because of the following issue:
#Chain 4 Exception: WBA_model_namespace::log_prob: external_weighting is nan, but must be greater than or equal to 0.000000 (in '/var/folders/7q/nqnlmy5d1_15vrk6dv1x103h0000gn/T/Rtmp60JPlJ/model-2d4472e52049.stan', line 17, column 2 to column 55)
#Chain 4 If this warning occurs sporadically, such as for highly constrained variable types like covariance matrices, then the sampler is fine,
#Chain 4 but if this warning occurs often then your model may be either severely ill-conditioned or misspecified.
```

```{r}
## Checking divergences

diagnostics$divergent__ |> sum()
```

```{r fig.width=12}

## Prior posterior update

plot_prior_posterior_update <- function(draws, title="") {
  ggplot(draws, aes(x = value, fill = source, colour = source)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.33, linewidth = 0.7) +
    facet_grid2(rows=vars(ID), cols=vars(parameter), scales = "free", independent = "y") +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = NULL, y = "Density", fill = NULL, colour = NULL
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3))
}

plot_prior_posterior_update(draws |> filter(model == "PBA", parameter %in% c("p")), title="PBA participant-level prior–posterior update")
plot_prior_posterior_update(draws |> filter(model == "WBA", parameter %in% c("rho", "kappa")), title="WBA participant-level prior–posterior update")
```

```{r fig.height=10}

## Prior and posterior predictive checks

collapsed_preds <- predictions |> summarise(
  .by = c(ID, FaceID, model),
  prior_pred=median(prior_pred, na.rm=TRUE),
  posterior_pred=median(posterior_pred, na.rm=TRUE),
  FirstRating=first(FirstRating),
  GroupRating=first(GroupRating),
  SecondRating=first(SecondRating),
) |> drop_na(ID, FaceID, FirstRating, GroupRating, SecondRating, prior_pred, posterior_pred)

plot_feedback_change_relation(collapsed_preds |> mutate(Feedback=GroupRating-FirstRating, Change=prior_pred-FirstRating), title="Prior predictive checks")
plot_feedback_change_relation(collapsed_preds |> mutate(Feedback=GroupRating-FirstRating, Change=posterior_pred-FirstRating), title="Posterior predictive checks")
```


```{r}

## Model comparison using loo weights

loo_per_fit <- map(fits, \(f) f$loo())

participant_ids <- unique(cogsci_data_observations$ID)
model_names     <- names(models)
n_models        <- length(model_names)

stacking_results <- tibble()

for (i in seq_along(participant_ids)) {
  idx <- ((i - 1) * n_models + 1):(i * n_models)
  loo_list <- loo_per_fit[idx]
  names(loo_list) <- model_names

  w <- loo_model_weights(loo_list, method = "stacking")

  stacking_results <- bind_rows(
    stacking_results,
    tibble(model = names(w), weight = as.numeric(w), ID = participant_ids[i])
  )
}

## Visualization 

stacking_results |>
  filter(model == "PBA") |>
  mutate(
    ID = factor(ID, levels = unique(cogsci_data_observations$ID)),
    favours = if_else(weight >= 0.5, "PBA", "WBA")
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = weight - 0.5, y = ID, colour = favours)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = weight - 0.5, yend = ID),
               linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(PBA = "#534AB7", WBA = "#0F6E56")) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = \(x) scales::percent(x + 0.5),
    breaks = c(-0.5, -0.25, 0, 0.25, 0.5),
    limits = c(-0.55, 0.55)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "PBA stacking weight", y = NULL, colour = "Favours",
    title = "Model comparison per participant",
    caption = "Dashed line = equal"
  ) 

```

```{r}

## traceplots for the fits 

model_params <- list(
  PBA = "p",
  WBA = c("rho", "kappa")
)

participant_ids <- unique(cogsci_data_observations$ID)
model_names <- c("PBA", "WBA")

pba_plots <- list()
wba_plots <- list()

for (i in seq_along(participant_ids)) {
  for (j in seq_along(model_names)) {
    idx   <- (i - 1) * length(model_names) + j
    fit   <- fits[[idx]]
    np    <- nuts_params(fit)
    model <- model_names[j]
    
    p <- bayesplot::mcmc_trace(
      fit$draws(format = "array"),
      pars = model_params[[model]],
      np   = np
    ) + ggtitle(paste(model, "-", participant_ids[i]))
    
    if (model == "PBA") pba_plots[[i]] <- p
    if (model == "WBA") wba_plots[[i]] <- p
  }
}

wrap_plots(pba_plots, ncol = 2)
wrap_plots(wba_plots, ncol = 2, width = 40)
```





```{r save-plots}
dir.create("plots", showWarnings = FALSE)

p_feedback_change_sim <- plot_feedback_change_relation(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))
ggsave("plots/feedback_change_simulation.png", p_feedback_change_sim, width = 6, height = 6)

p_participant_behaviour_sim <- plot_participant_behaviour(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))
ggsave("plots/participant_behaviour_simulation.png", p_participant_behaviour_sim, width = 10, height = 5)

p_rating_distributions <- add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols = c(FirstRating, GroupRating, SecondRating),
  names_to = "Observation", values_to = "Rating"
) |> ggplot(aes(x = Rating)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 8)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(ID), cols = vars(Observation)) +
  labs(y = "Observation count")
ggsave("plots/rating_distributions.png", p_rating_distributions, width = 8, height = 4)

p_offset_distributions <- add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols = c(Feedback, Change),
  names_to = "Observation", values_to = "Offset"
) |> ggplot(aes(x = Offset)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-7, 7), limits = c(-7, 7)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(ID), cols = vars(factor(Observation, c("Feedback", "Change")))) +
  labs(y = "Observation count")
ggsave("plots/offset_distributions.png", p_offset_distributions, width = 8, height = 4)

for (m in unique(results_all$ModelType)) {
  p <- results_all |>
    filter(ModelType == m) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SecondRating, fill = AgentType)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "dodge", colour = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~ AgentType) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
    labs(title = paste("Distribution of Second Ratings:", m),
         x = "Second Rating", y = "Count", fill = "Agent Type") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  ggsave(paste0("plots/second_ratings_", tolower(m), ".png"), p, width = 10, height = 5)
}

np_w_local <- bayesplot::nuts_params(fit_w)
np_p_local <- bayesplot::nuts_params(fit_p)

p_trace_wba <- bayesplot::mcmc_trace(fit_w$draws(format = "array"), pars = c("rho", "kappa"), np = np_w_local)
ggsave("plots/trace_wba.png", p_trace_wba, width = 10, height = 5)

p_trace_pba <- bayesplot::mcmc_trace(fit_p$draws(format = "array"), pars = "p", np = np_p_local)
ggsave("plots/trace_pba.png", p_trace_pba, width = 8, height = 4)

p_confusion_matrix <- binary_matrix |>
  ggplot(aes(x = predicted_model, y = true_model, fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(proportion, accuracy = 1)), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  labs(title = "Model Recovery - Confusion matrix",
       x = "Winning Candidate Model (Max Weight)", y = "True Generating Model", fill = "Win Rate") +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
ggsave("plots/model_recovery_confusion_matrix.png", p_confusion_matrix, width = 6, height = 5)

p_participant_behaviour_cogsci <- plot_participant_behaviour(add_diagnostic_columns(cogsci_data_observations))
ggsave("plots/participant_behaviour_cogsci.png", p_participant_behaviour_cogsci, width = 10, height = 12)

p_prior_posterior_pba <- plot_prior_posterior_update(draws |> filter(model == "PBA", parameter %in% c("p")), title = "PBA participant-level prior–posterior update")
ggsave("plots/prior_posterior_pba.png", p_prior_posterior_pba, width = 12, height = 8)

p_prior_posterior_wba <- plot_prior_posterior_update(draws |> filter(model == "WBA", parameter %in% c("rho", "kappa")), title = "WBA participant-level prior–posterior update")
ggsave("plots/prior_posterior_wba.png", p_prior_posterior_wba, width = 12, height = 8)

p_prior_pred_checks <- plot_feedback_change_relation(collapsed_preds |> mutate(Feedback = GroupRating - FirstRating, Change = prior_pred - FirstRating), title = "Prior predictive checks")
ggsave("plots/prior_predictive_checks.png", p_prior_pred_checks, width = 10, height = 10)

p_posterior_pred_checks <- plot_feedback_change_relation(collapsed_preds |> mutate(Feedback = GroupRating - FirstRating, Change = posterior_pred - FirstRating), title = "Posterior predictive checks")
ggsave("plots/posterior_predictive_checks.png", p_posterior_pred_checks, width = 10, height = 10)
```
